tossups.question: A character created by this author refuses to believe his brother volunteered himself to be killed during a World War II raid. This author wrote about a young girl abandoned in a warehouse who dies after a dog bite. In that novel by this author, 15 boys at a reformatory school are abandoned in a plague-stricken village and are taught by Li. A man created by this author reveals his (*) mentally disabled sister committed suicide after he slept with her. In that novel by this author, an American youth football team loots a supermarket owned by a Korean man named “The Emperor” under orders from Takashi and Mitsu. A semi-autobiographical novel about a father whose son has a brain hernia is by, for 10 points, what Japanese author of The Silent Cry and A Personal Matter?
tossups.question_sanitized: A character created by this author refuses to believe his brother volunteered himself to be killed during a World War II raid. This author wrote about a young girl abandoned in a warehouse who dies after a dog bite. In that novel by this author, 15 boys at a reformatory school are abandoned in a plague-stricken village and are taught by Li. A man created by this author reveals his (*) mentally disabled sister committed suicide after he slept with her. In that novel by this author, an American youth football team loots a supermarket owned by a Korean man named "The Emperor" under orders from Takashi and Mitsu. A semi-autobiographical novel about a father whose son has a brain hernia is by, for 10 points, what Japanese author of The Silent Cry and A Personal Matter?

tossups.question: This man’s first published work was a short story called “Lavish was the dead”. In a novel by this author, a character paints his face crimson and hangs himself. This man’s Seventeen and Death of a Political Youth was inspired by the assassination of the chairman of a socialist party. This author’s first novel tells of (*) 15 teenage boys in a reformatory school. That novel, Nip the Buds, Shoot the Kids ends with the protagonist Li being chased into the forest by villagers, with his fate left ambiguous. A semi-autobiographical novel by this author tells the story of a father who must come to terms with his mentally disabled newborn son. For 10 points, name this Japanese author of A Personal Matter who passed away in 2023.
tossups.question_sanitized: This man's first published work was a short story called "Lavish was the dead". In a novel by this author, a character paints his face crimson and hangs himself. This man's Seventeen and Death of a Political Youth was inspired by the assassination of the chairman of a socialist party. This author's first novel tells of (*) 15 teenage boys in a reformatory school. That novel, Nip the Buds, Shoot the Kids ends with the protagonist Li being chased into the forest by villagers, with his fate left ambiguous. A semi-autobiographical novel by this author tells the story of a father who must come to terms with his mentally disabled newborn son. For 10 points, name this Japanese author of A Personal Matter who passed away in 2023.

tossups.question: Description acceptable. In one novel, the expressionless gaze of a character based on this person is compared to the appearance of a man who falsely claimed to have raped and murdered a local teenage girl. This person, whose early life is detailed in the memoir A Healing Family, was the inspiration for a character who appears as "a fat baby in a white cotton nightgown" and as "big as a kangaroo" to a suicidal composer who kills himself by jumping into (*) traffic. In another novel, the birth of a character based on this person and the suicide of a man who painted his face crimson are the driving forces for Mitsu's depression. The protagonist Bird incorrectly believes that a character based on this person is suffering from a brain hernia in the novel A Personal Matter. For 10 points, name or describe this person who provided the inspiration for several disabled infants in novels by Kenzaburo Oe.
tossups.answer: Hikari Oe [accept any answer indicating the son of Kenzaburo Oe; prompt on just Oe]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Hikari Oe [accept any answer indicating the son of Kenzaburo Oe; prompt on just Oe]
tossups.question_sanitized: Description acceptable. The protagonist Bird incorrectly believes that a character based on this person is suffering from a brain hernia in the novel A Personal Matter. For 10 points, name or describe this person who provided the inspiration for several disabled infants in novels by Kenzaburo Oe.

tossups.question: The unnamed narrator of a novel by this author is a university student who has a neurotic fear of syphilis and of sex. A character created by this author rose from his roots as a slave laborer in Shikoku to become the richest man in his village. In a novel by this author, the brother of a one-eyed former English professor begins organizing the youth of his home village by teaching them how to play American football. In that novel by this author of Outcries, the (*) Emperor's supermarket is looted, partially because he is ethnically Korean. In a semi-autobiographical novel by this author, Bird comes to grips with raising his son, who was born with a brain hernia. For 10 points, name this Japanese Nobel laureate, who wrote The Silent Cry and A Personal Matter.
tossups.question_sanitized: The unnamed narrator of a novel by this author is a university student who has a neurotic fear of syphilis and of sex. A character created by this author rose from his roots as a slave laborer in Shikoku to become the richest man in his village. In a novel by this author, the brother of a one-eyed former English professor begins organizing the youth of his home village by teaching them how to play American football. In that novel by this author of Outcries, the (*) Emperor's supermarket is looted, partially because he is ethnically Korean. In a semi-autobiographical novel by this author, Bird comes to grips with raising his son, who was born with a brain hernia. For 10 points, name this Japanese Nobel laureate, who wrote The Silent Cry and A Personal Matter.

tossups.question: A character created by this author becomes the companion to a banker’s son because he needs money to buy an annotated edition of Romain Rolland’s L’Ame Enchante . In a story by this writer, an obese man names his child after a word translating as “forest.” Musical imagery in a story by this man includes a Bach Cantata that is sung by some rebels and the song (*) “Happy Days are Here Again,” which the narrator sings to himself while wearing his father’s goggles and waiting for a cancer diagnosis. He wrote a story in which a boy nicknamed Eeyore is confined to an asylum and a story in which the composer D imagines seeing the image of his dead child in the heavens. For 10 points, name this author of The Day He Himself Shall Wipe My Tears Away , a collection that includes “Aghwee the Sky Monster.”
tossups.question_sanitized: A character created by this author becomes the companion to a banker's son because he needs money to buy an annotated edition of Romain Rolland's L'Ame Enchante . In a story by this writer, an obese man names his child after a word translating as "forest." Musical imagery in a story by this man includes a Bach Cantata that is sung by some rebels and the song (*) "Happy Days are Here Again," which the narrator sings to himself while wearing his father's goggles and waiting for a cancer diagnosis. He wrote a story in which a boy nicknamed Eeyore is confined to an asylum and a story in which the composer D imagines seeing the image of his dead child in the heavens. For 10 points, name this author of The Day He Himself Shall Wipe My Tears Away , a collection that includes "Aghwee the Sky Monster."

tossups.question: One character created by this author listens to a broadcast about Soviet nuclear testing while agonizing over his decision to dilute the milk being fed to his infant son. In a novel on similar themes, this author created a character who writes the play Gulliver’s Foot and the Country of the Little People, remembers almost drowning in Carp Cave, and looks to the poetry of William Blake to help raise his son. In the first-mentioned novel by this author, the protagonist is fired from a cram school after showing up drunk and decides not to go to Africa so that he can care for his son, who was born with a brain hernia. K tends to his son, Eeyore, in Rouse Up O Young Men of the New Age, a novel by, for 10 points, what Japanese author who wrote about a man called Bird in his novel A Personal Matter?
tossups.question_sanitized: One character created by this author listens to a broadcast about Soviet nuclear testing while agonizing over his decision to dilute the milk being fed to his infant son. In a novel on similar themes, this author created a character who writes the play Gulliver's Foot and the Country of the Little People, remembers almost drowning in Carp Cave, and looks to the poetry of William Blake to help raise his son. In the first-mentioned novel by this author, the protagonist is fired from a cram school after showing up drunk and decides not to go to Africa so that he can care for his son, who was born with a brain hernia. K tends to his son, Eeyore, in Rouse Up O Young Men of the New Age, a novel by, for 10 points, what Japanese author who wrote about a man called Bird in his novel A Personal Matter?

tossups.question: In a novel by this man the protagonist makes a fool of himself by registering a very weak punch on a boxing game in the arcade, only to beat up the very thugs who upstaged him on that game. That character collects maps of Africa in the hopes of one day travelling there. A recurring motif in works of this author is a child born with brain damage. For 10 points, name this Japanese Nobel laureate, the author of A Personal Matter and Silent Cry.
tossups.question_sanitized: In a novel by this man the protagonist makes a fool of himself by registering a very weak punch on a boxing game in the arcade, only to beat up the very thugs who upstaged him on that game. That character collects maps of Africa in the hopes of one day travelling there. A recurring motif in works of this author is a child born with brain damage. For 10 points, name this Japanese Nobel laureate, the author of A Personal Matter and Silent Cry.

bonuses.leadin: In The Tale of Genji, one of these animals named Okinamaro is temporarily exiled from the palace until the Emperor forgives it after it starts crying. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In The Tale of Genji, one of these animals named Okinamaro is temporarily exiled from the palace until the Emperor forgives it after it starts crying. For 10 points each:
parts: What animals also name a group of warriors that title a Kyokutei Bakin novel set during the Ashikaga period that is considered the longest novel in Japanese history?

The narrator falls in love with a girl who dies after contracting the plague from the village dog Leo in this author’s novel Nip the Buds, Shoot the Kids. This author also wrote the semi-autobiographical novel A Personal Matter.

A dog named after Charles Lindbergh is gifted to Hiroshi, the son of Kaname and Misako in this Jun’ichirō Tanizaki novel. Kaname gets drawn into his father-in-law’s obsession with Bunraku theater in this novel.
parts_sanitized: What animals also name a group of warriors that title a Kyokutei Bakin novel set during the Ashikaga period that is considered the longest novel in Japanese history?

The narrator falls in love with a girl who dies after contracting the plague from the village dog Leo in this author's novel Nip the Buds, Shoot the Kids. This author also wrote the semi-autobiographical novel A Personal Matter.

A dog named after Charles Lindbergh is gifted to Hiroshi, the son of Kaname and Misako in this Jun'ichiro Tanizaki novel. Kaname gets drawn into his father-in-law's obsession with Bunraku theater in this novel.
answers: dogs [accept Nansō Satomi Hakkenden, The Eight Dog Chronicles, Tale of Eight Dogs, or Biographies of Eight Dogs; accept inu]
answers_sanitized: dogs [accept Nanso Satomi Hakkenden, The Eight Dog Chronicles, Tale of Eight Dogs, or Biographies of Eight Dogs; accept inu]
Kenzaburo Oe ("OH-ay")
Some Prefer Nettles [accept Tade ku mushi]

packet.name: M - Columbia A, Ohio State A, Maryland A, Toronto C, Clemson A, Notre Dame A, Texas A

bonuses.leadin: In one novel about people of this ethnicity, the siblings Mozasu and Noa run the title gaming establishments and the central family emigrates after a local crime boss conceives Noa during an extramarital affair. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this ethnicity of Takao, the hero of Outcries, as well as of the inhabitants of a settlement that S dies attacking, who include a shopkeeper whose supermarket is looted by a band of soccer-playing youth.
The grocery store-owning Emperor appears in this novel in which Takashi shoots himself and the Emperor destroys Mitsusaburo's shop before he leaves on a wildlife expedition to Africa.
The Silent Cry and Outcries were written by this Japanese Nobel laureate who also wrote novels including A Personal Matter and Nip the Buds, Shoot the Kids.
answers: The Silent Cry [accept Man'en Gannen no Futtoboru, Football on the First Day of Man'en]
answers_sanitized: Korean-Japanese [accept Chosenjin, Hanminjok]
The Silent Cry [accept Man'en Gannen no Futtoboru, Football on the First Day of Man'en]
Kenzaburo Oe

bonuses.leadin: A “faint scar” is the only remainder of this deformity after a surgery is successfully performed on Hassan in Khaled Hosseini’s novel The Kite Runner. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this physical deformity of a title character who builds a rickshaw to carry his sick mother to Prince Albert, and who is later taken by soldiers to a rehabilitation camp.
A character known as Hare-Lip refuses to reveal his name to Rubashov, who is a fellow prisoner held and interrogated by “Number One” in this novel named for an event during Christ’s crucifixion.
Another character called Harelip wardens a captive airman in “Prize Stock,” a short story by this Nobel Prize-winning Japanese author of A Personal Matter and The Silent Cry.
answers: hare lip [or cleft lip or cleft palate] (The latter novel is Life and Times of Michael K.)
answers_sanitized: hare lip [or cleft lip or cleft palate] (The latter novel is Life and Times of Michael K.)
Darkness at Noon [or Sonnerfinsternis] (by Arthur Koestler)
Kenzaburo Oe [accept names in either order]

bonuses.leadin: The first of these people, Alfred Birnbaum, initially created teaching editions for Kodansha, while another of them, Jay Rubin, wrote a 2002 book on a certain author “and the Music of Words”. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this group of people whose common task was most recently carried out by Philip Gabriel, resulting in the 2014 Knopf publication of Colorless Tsukuru Tazaki and His Years of Pilgrimage.

Gabriel won the 2006 PEN Translation Prize for his English edition of this Murakami novel, whose chapters alternate between the perspectives of the lost cat-hunter Nakata and the title character, a 15-year-old runaway.

Gabriel also translated Somersault, a novel by this second-ever Japanese lit Nobel laureate. Many of his novels, including A Personal Matter and The Silent Cry, include fictional portrayals of his own handicapped son Hikari.
answers_sanitized: English translators of Haruki Murakami [or obvious equivalents]
Kafka on the Shore [or Umibe no Kafuka]
Kenzaburo Oe [accept names in either order]

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, Himiko is part white because her grandfather married a Russian from Vladivostok. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which Bird comes to terms with his son's brain hernia.
This Japanese author of Nip the Buds, Shoot the Kids and The Silent Cry sublimated his experiences with his son Hikari to write A Personal Matter.
Oe's The Silent Cry is narrated by this one-eyed English professor married to Natsumi.
answers_sanitized: A Personal Matter [or Kojinteki na Taiken]
Kenzaburo Oe
Mitsusaburo

bonuses.leadin: For 10 points each, name some writers from very different parts of the world who wrote about sky monsters.
parts: This Uruguayan author wrote about a man that slowly loses his wife to a parasite of feathered creatures, after that parasite finds its way into the title feather pillow and begins to feed on her.

This author wrote about “a fat baby […] big as a kangaroo” that harasses his father in Agwhee the Sky Monster. He wrote about a man who sodomizes himself with a cucumber before committing suicide in another work.
John William Polidori’s The Vampyre probably at least partially inspired this Irishman’s gothic novel Dracula.
answers_sanitized: Horacio Quiroga
Kenzaburo Oe
Bram Stoker

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, a group of children are transferred away from a correctional facility in a city to avoid air raids. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which the children are trapped in a village full of animals killed by an epidemic and are shot at when they try to escape.
This other novel by the same author follows the brothers Mitsusaburo and Takashi, including the latter’s revolt against the Emperor. Its author won the 1967 Tanizaki Prize.
Nip the Buds, Shoot the Kids and The Silent Cry were written by this Japanese novelist of A Personal Matter who was influenced by his brain-damaged son Hikari.
answers: Nip the Buds, Shoot the Kids [or Memushiri Ko-Uchi]
answers_sanitized: Nip the Buds, Shoot the Kids [or Memushiri Ko-Uchi]
The Silent Cry [or Man'en Gan'nen no Futtoboru]
Kenzaburo Oe
